mappings.rationaleAI: Biological inheritance addresses biological basis (A.1), twin studies and chromosomes (A.2), genetics applications (A.3), behavioural traits (A.4), and race concepts (B.1, B.2).
Genetic variation, sex linkage, and mutations address biological basis (A.1), genetic abnormalities (A.2.b), forensic applications (A.3), heritability of IQ (A.4), and human diversity (B.1).
Genome assembly addresses biological basis (A.1) and personalized medicine applications (A.3).
DNA structure addresses biological basis (A.1) and DNA fingerprinting applications (A.3).
Development from fertilized egg addresses biological basis (A.1), and identical twins relate to twin studies (A.2.a).
Population genetics and dispersal patterns address human physical variation (B) and diversity (B.1).
Natural selection shaping variation addresses human diversity (B.1).
Twin studies and genetic contributions address twin inferences (A.2.a) and behavioural trait inheritance (A.4).
Eugenic policies address genetics applications (A.3) and race measures (B.2).
Biological racism addresses human physical variation (B) and race measures (B.2).
Nature-nurture and species concepts address heredity (A) and physical variation (B).
